title: Number input component with increment/decrement controls
purpose: Provides a number input field with built-in increment and decrement buttons, supporting min/max constraints and controlled/uncontrolled value states.
module_context: Exports a React functional component named NumberInput that accepts various props for configuration and handles internal state management for uncontrolled inputs.

steps.content: Generate a unique ID for the input element.
Initialize internal state for the input value, defaulting to the controlled value or empty string.
Determine the current value to display, using the controlled value if provided, otherwise the internal state.
Define a clamping function to constrain values within min/max bounds.
Set up the change handler to update internal state and notify parent of changes.
Set up the step handler to increment or decrement the value with clamping.
Render the label, decrement button, input field, and increment button with appropriate styling and event handlers.
Initialize result with the input value.
IF a minimum value is defined and the result is below it, set the result to the minimum.
IF a maximum value is defined and the result is above it, set the result to the maximum.
Return the clamped result.
Extract the raw value from the input element.
IF the raw value is empty, clear the internal state and notify parent with undefined.
ELSE parse the raw value to a number.
IF the parsed value is not a number, do nothing.
ELSE clamp the parsed value within min/max bounds.
Update internal state with the clamped value.
Notify parent of the new clamped value.
IF the input is disabled, do nothing.
Determine the current value, defaulting to zero if not a number.
Calculate the new value by adding the step amount in the specified direction.
Clamp the new value within min/max bounds.
Update internal state with the clamped new value.
Notify parent of the new clamped value.
